venin.coverCaption: The double vinyl of the album "Le Premier Sang" is available on Bandcamp.
venin.intro1: Venin is an iconic French rock band, born in Marseille in 1983. Blending the raw energy of hard rock with heavy and blues influences, Venin carved its name on the national scene thanks to French-language lyrics and a unique musical identity. After a demo tape in 1984 and a well-received first single two years later, the band lined up concerts, regularly sharing the bill with major players of the French rock scene.
venin.intro2: Despite a hiatus at the end of the decade, Venin was reborn in the 2010s, reconnecting with its loyal fanbase and a renewed creative drive. Their discography, marked by authenticity and striking energy, makes them an emblematic figure of French hard rock.
venin.intro3: For their latest album, "Le Premier Sang", I had the pleasure of welcoming Venin to Global Drip Studio, where I handled the editing, mixing and mastering of the 9 new tracks, as well as the recording of vocals, solo parts, and some keyboard arrangements, notably on the track "Dis moi si c'est beau".
venin.eightiesTitle: The album was entirely designed to sound eighties:
venin.eighties1: Compositions typical of the golden years of hard/heavy metal. It even includes a cover of the French band Slaughter's "Excalibur", originally released in 1986.
venin.eighties2: All musicians were recorded at the same time, networked in the Simone Veil hall in Châteauneuf-les-Martigues, by Mathieu Battini; as mentioned earlier, only the vocals (and a few solos) were recorded afterwards at Global Drip Studio.
venin.eighties3: No parts were copy-pasted. Not a single chorus, drum fill, or even a bass line.
venin.eighties4: No trigger was used. Rare enough in 2025 to be worth mentioning, but that was the band's choice, which greatly contributes to the color of the project.
venin.eighties5: Microphones ranging from classic to high-end were used. Guitars were recorded through SM57s, vocals with Griffon Microphones, using the already near-classic T12 (clone of the legendary AKG C12)/Fuzzyphone pairing. The DI bass was re-amped through an Ampeg emulation.
venin.technicalTitle: Here are some additional technical details about the recording:
venin.technicalP1: Rhythm guitars were recorded through three different amp heads: ENGL GigMaster for Lionel, and Marshall JVM410H + Hughes&Kettner GranMeister for Jean-Marc. Solo parts were recorded DI, then emulated through Guitar Rig, to easily seat the lead parts within powerful rhythm tracks.
venin.technicalP2: The bonus track "Extraball" was composed by Mathieu Battini (and performed by himself alongside Lionel Bredenbach). Guitars were recorded at Global Drip Studio, using double tracking.
venin.technicalChain1: E-II Standard Horizon (EMG pickups) → Orange Rockerverb MkIII → Unison Pre → Sennheiser e906
venin.technicalChain2: E-II Standard Horizon (EMG pickups) → EVH III 5150 Ivory 50w → Neve 1073 Pre → Shure SM57
venin.technicalVocals: Regarding the vocals, the GMT12 was paired with a Neve 1073 and a General Audio Research Sonar compressor, all in 500 series format. Add the Griffon Microphones Fuzzyphone, some in-the-box processing, and you get this texture that's both velvety and trashy. Jean-Marc's backing vocals were also recorded this way, but Hélène's were only recorded with the T12 to bring some clarity.
venin.technicalDrums: Gerald's drum kit is a Ludwig, and the snare is a Gretsch 4"x6.5" Black Hammered Snare.
venin.outro: Venin continues its musical adventure today, as passionate and independent as ever, true to the spirit of French rock.
venin.logoCaption: The iconic logo of the band Venin
venin.availableOn: The album "Le Premier Sang" is available for streaming on all platforms, as well as on CD and vinyl, notably on the band's Bandcamp at:

venin.bringToLifeDesc: Recording, mixing, mastering — contact us to discuss your project and get a personalized quote.

compression.title: Understanding compression in five minutes
compression.subtitle: Compression demystified: ratio, attack, release, knee. Learn to use this essential tool to control the dynamics of your recordings.
compression.meta: Global Drip Studio • December 10, 2024 • 5 min read
compression.intro: Compression is one of those tools that separate a merely correct sound from a truly controlled, readable and professional one. In the studio, it stabilizes a performance, tames level variations, and places a source at the right distance in the mix. It can be invisible when properly set, or become an aesthetic choice when you want to commit to a character.
compression.keyIdea: If you should remember just one idea, it's this: a compressor doesn't magically make a sound better — it makes a sound more controllable. And when a signal is controllable, it sits better, sounds better, and integrates better with everything else.
compression.readAsPhrase: To understand compression quickly, stop seeing it as a mysterious black box and read it as a simple sentence: from a certain level, the compressor acts, more or less strongly, more or less quickly, and more or less gently.
compression.thresholdP1: Before even talking about ratio, you need to understand the threshold. The threshold is the level at which compression starts working. As long as the signal stays below it, the compressor does nothing. As soon as it exceeds it, the level is reduced according to the settings.
compression.thresholdP2: The threshold answers a very concrete question: do you only want to catch the peaks that exceed it, or do you want to control a good part of the performance? The lower the threshold, the more often the compressor intervenes, and the more present the control effect becomes.
compression.ratioTitle: The ratio, the amount of control
compression.ratioP1: The ratio tells how much the compressor reduces what exceeds the threshold. A gentle ratio gives subtle control. A higher ratio gives firmer control, with a generally more obvious effect.
compression.ratioP2: Think of it as a promise: once the signal has crossed the threshold, the ratio decides the severity. It's a behavior setting, not a volume setting. If you're looking for transparency, a moderate ratio is often easier to make musical. If you need a very controlled source, a higher ratio can help, but it requires more careful listening to avoid freezing the performance.
compression.attackTitle: The attack, how the transient is caught
compression.attackP1: The attack is the speed at which the compressor reacts when the signal exceeds the threshold. It's one of the most influential settings on the feeling of punch and precision.
compression.attackP2: When the attack is fast, the compressor immediately catches the beginning of the sound. This can smooth transients and give a tighter, sometimes denser feel. When the attack is slower, the very beginning of the sound has time to pass before the reduction kicks in. This preserves more of the natural impact and presence.
compression.attackP3: In the studio, this setting is felt more than measured. If a source loses its energy and relief, the attack is often too fast. If on the contrary the attacks remain too aggressive and stick out of the mix, the attack may be too slow, or the threshold too high for the compressor to do its job.
compression.releaseP1: The release is the speed at which the compressor lets go of the reduction after compressing. It gives the feeling of breathing, movement, or sometimes pumping.
compression.releaseP2: A release that's too short can make the processing nervous and audible, especially on bass-heavy sounds. A release that's too long can hold back the source, as if it never returns to its natural level, which can flatten the musicality.
compression.releaseP3: The right release follows the phrasing and dynamics of the track. When it's well set, compression feels natural. When it's poorly set, you hear level variations that have nothing to do with the musical intention.
compression.kneeTitle: The knee, the softness of compression onset
compression.kneeP1: The knee describes how compression settles around the threshold. With a soft knee, the transition is gradual, compression arrives more discreetly. With a harder knee, the transition is sharp, which can give a more assertive effect.
compression.kneeP2: For a voice or source you want to control without drawing attention to the processing, a soft knee is often easier to integrate. For a sound that needs to be more incisive and assertive, a harder knee can be a relevant choice. Again, it's not about better or worse, but about intention.
compression.compareTitle: The right reflex in the studio: compare at equal level
compression.compareP1: Compression often gives the impression that it's better simply because it's louder after processing. To judge correctly, you need to compare at equal level, adjusting the output gain.
compression.compareP2: At that point, you hear the real difference, the one that matters: is the source more stable? Does it sit better? Is the diction more readable? Is the playing more consistent? A well-set compressor gives a sense of mastery. It reduces surprises without removing life.
compression.summaryTitle: What you now know in five minutes
compression.summaryP1: You can read a compressor as simple logic: the threshold decides when it acts, the ratio decides how much it acts, the attack decides if it lets the transient through, the release decides how it lets go, the knee decides how it enters action.
compression.summaryP2: From there, compression becomes a normal studio tool, just like good gain staging, placement in the processing chain, or a bus decision. And that's exactly what it should be: a reliable lever to control the dynamics of your recordings, tracks and mix.
compression.ctaTitle: Want a studio-quality result on your takes or mix?
compression.ctaDesc: If you want to go faster and further, the most effective approach is to work on real material, in a studio environment, with reliable monitoring and committed decisions. For a recording session, mixing or mastering, you can book or contact me directly.

voix.title: How to mix vocals: the 7 essential steps
voix.subtitle: Master the art of vocal mixing with these professional techniques used in our studio
voix.meta: Global Drip Studio • December 15, 2024 • 6 min read
voix.intro: The voice is the central element of most music productions. A successful vocal mix can transform an ordinary song into a memorable hit. Here are the 7 essential steps we apply at Global Drip Studio.
voix.step1Desc: Before any processing, remove parasitic noises, unwanted breaths and correct timing. Use a noise gate to eliminate background noise between phrases. This step determines the baseline quality of your mix.
voix.step2Desc: EQ is your best ally for shaping vocal timbre:
step2Items: 200-800 Hz: Critical zone for warmth and boxiness
voix.step3Desc: Compression controls dynamics and ensures consistency. Use a ratio of 3:1 to 6:1, with a slow attack (10-30ms) to preserve transients and a release adapted to tempo (100-300ms). Aim for 3-6 dB of gain reduction.
voix.step4Desc: Attenuate sibilants (S, T, F) without dulling the voice. Target the 4-8 kHz range with a multiband de-esser or dynamic EQ. The goal: reduce hissing while preserving clarity.
voix.step5Desc: Add color with subtle saturation. Tube or tape emulation brings warmth and character. Stay subtle: 5-15% saturation is enough to enrich the timbre without denaturing the voice.
step6Items: Reverb: Hall, plate or room depending on desired ambiance
Delay: Subtle echo (1/8 or 1/4 note) for thickness
voix.step7Desc: Position the voice at the center of the stereo panorama and find the optimal level relative to instruments. Use automation to maintain consistent presence and create intensity variations across song sections.
voix.proTipTitle: Pro tip from Global Drip Studio
voix.proTipDesc: Vocal mixing is an art that requires experience and quality references. Every voice is unique and needs a personalized approach. Don't hesitate to experiment and trust your ears.

sounddesign.title: 10 sound design techniques to create unique atmospheres
sounddesign.subtitle: Explore advanced sound creation techniques for immersive and original sonic worlds
sounddesign.meta: Global Drip Studio • December 5, 2024 • 9 min read
sounddesign.intro: Sound design is not just about dressing up a production: it creates emotions, transports the listener and gives a unique identity to your work.
sounddesign.introSub: Here are 10 professional techniques we use at Global Drip Studio to create impactful sonic worlds.
sounddesign.t1Desc: Capture real-world sounds to create authentic and organic textures.
t1Items: Manipulate playback speed for dramatic effects
Layer multiple takes to enrich texture
sounddesign.t2Desc: Decompose and recompose sound at the microscopic level for evolving textures.
sounddesign.t3Desc: Use impulse responses from real spaces to place your sounds in impossible environments.
sounddesign.t3Quote: "Imagine making a voice resonate in a Gothic cathedral, an industrial hangar, or even... a grand piano!"
sounddesign.t4Desc: Manipulate frequency content in real-time for radical transformations.
t4Items: Spectral freeze: Freeze harmonics in time
Spectral shift: Shift formants for alien voices
sounddesign.t5Desc: Use LFOs, envelopes and sequencers to animate your sonic textures.
sounddesign.t6Desc: Beyond classic saturation, explore alternative distortion modes.
sounddesign.t7Desc: Create immersive 3D experiences for headphones and surround systems.
t7Items: Distance modeling with filtering and delay
sounddesign.t8Desc: Exploit the peculiarities of human hearing to create sonic illusions.
t8Items: Frequency masking: Hide elements within others
sounddesign.t9Desc: Create controlled loops to generate evolving and complex textures.
sounddesign.t9Warning: ⚠️ Warning: always control gain to avoid destructive saturation!
sounddesign.t10Desc: Gradually transform one sound into another for organic transitions.
t10Items: Spectral morphing: Interpolation in the frequency domain
Envelope following: Dynamics transfer between sources
sounddesign.conclusionTitle: The art of sound design at Global Drip Studio
sounddesign.conclusionDesc: These techniques are just tools. The real magic comes from experimentation, creative intuition and attentive listening. At Global Drip Studio, we combine technical expertise and artistic vision to create sonic worlds that leave their mark.
sounddesign.conclusionQuote: "Sound design is painting with sound. Every texture tells a story, every atmosphere evokes an emotion."
sounddesign.ctaButton: Collaborate on your sound design project

zeu.description: Creative collaborations with a renowned underground rap artist with authentic flows.
zeu.collaborationDetails: I composed and recorded 'JDAY', as well as recorded 'J'DEVRAIS' and 'RARE' with ZEU. An intense creative collaboration that gave birth to authentic tracks blending catchy flows and polished productions.

tany.description: Privileged partnership and strong artistic relationship with custom direction.
tany.collaborationDetails: Privileged partnership with Tany, we've developed a strong and lasting artistic relationship. Together, we build a unique artistic direction that defines his musical identity.

lil-moine.description: Close collaboration since 2020, near-exclusive production of his tracks and audiovisual projects.
lil-moine.collaborationDetails: Privileged collaboration since 2020 with Lil Moine. I produce almost every track and project for him, creating a coherent and evolving sound. This lasting artistic relationship allows us to constantly explore new sonic territories while maintaining his strong musical identity.

eddy-de-mart.description: Mixing and mastering since 2022, bringing technical dimension to his creations.
eddy-de-mart.collaborationDetails: Since 2022, I handle the mixing and mastering of Eddy de Mart's projects. Our collaboration focuses on achieving a professional and impactful sound, showcasing his compositions with a rigorous technical approach.

black-beanie-dub.description: Project co-founded with Mathieu Battini in 2017, our most recognized achievement.
black-beanie-dub.collaborationDetails: Black Beanie Dub is a project I founded with Mathieu Battini in 2017. It became our most successful project, exploring the territories of dub and roots reggae, electronic music and sonic experimentation, always respecting the traditional foundations of the genre. An artistic adventure that continues to evolve.

lofai.description: Collaborative project exploring the intersection between artificial intelligence and lofi music.
lofai.collaborationDetails: LOFAI is a fascinating collaborative project exploring the intersection between artificial intelligence and lofi music. We work with talented musicians and exceptional jazz players — LOFAI wouldn't exist without them. We experiment with AI tools to create unique sonic textures while preserving the authenticity and warmth of traditional lofi.

ekzo.description: My beatmaker/producer alias since 2022 for my personal creative projects.
ekzo.collaborationDetails: Ekzo is my beatmaker/producer name since 2022. Under this alias, I release all my personal projects, exploring different styles and production approaches. It's my creative laboratory where I express my personal artistic vision.

lave.description: Promising young talent, mixing and mastering of the track 'Bête noir'.
lave.collaborationDetails: I had the pleasure of mixing and mastering 'Bête noir' for Lavé, a very talented young artist. The project required a delicate approach to preserve the authenticity of his voice while delivering the depth and impact needed.

mamood.description: Collaboration on the recording, mixing and mastering of Adios and MPLPVP.
mamood.collaborationDetails: I recorded and mixed 'Adios' and 'MPLPVP' for Mamood. These sessions were particularly enriching, exploring contemporary urban sounds with a precise technical approach to capture the energy of his performances.

theo-bachelier.description: Privileged collaboration around sound design, post-production and creative mixing.
theo-bachelier.collaborationDetails: Privileged collaboration with Théo Bachelier around sound design. I work on the post-production, sound design and mixing of his creative projects. An artistic synergy that allows us to push the boundaries of contemporary sound creation.

timothe-chatenoud.description: Self-produced artist with a unique musical identity, we bring the finishing touch to Timothé's creations with hybrid mastering.
timothe-chatenoud.collaborationDetails: Timothé Chatenoud is a self-produced artist with a strong and unique musical identity. We step in to bring the professional finishing touch to his creations, preserving his authenticity while optimizing sound quality.

venin.description: Iconic rock band from Southern France, recording, editing, mixing and mastering of the album 'Le Premier Sang'.
venin.collaborationDetails: For their album 'Le Premier Sang', I had the honor of welcoming Venin to Global Drip Studio. I handled the editing, mixing and mastering of the 9 new tracks, as well as the recording of vocals, solo parts, and some keyboard arrangements. A project entirely designed to sound eighties with an authentic approach — no copy-paste, no triggers.

ritualz.description: A powerful comeback in 2025 after ten years of experience, mixing and mastering his new productions.
ritualz.collaborationDetails: Ritualz is an explosive blend of hard-hitting rhythms, powerful bass and diverse sounds. After more than ten years of production experience but being away from the scene, he returns in 2025 with renewed energy, ready to rebuild his artistic project from scratch. We work alongside him on mixing and mastering.

jibbs-arenas.description: Swiss and Spanish artist, close collaboration from recording to artistic direction.
jibbs-arenas.collaborationDetails: Jibbs Arenas is a Swiss and Spanish artist who draws inspiration from his roots and adapts them to any style. We work in close collaboration at Global Drip Studio, from recording to mixing and mastering, including production.

privacy.s1text: Global Drip Studio collects personal data when you use our contact form or contact us by email. The data collected may include:
privacy.s1items:
• First and last name
• Email address
• Phone number
• Information related to your music project
privacy.s2text: The collected data is used exclusively for:
privacy.s2items:
• Responding to your inquiries
• Providing personalized quotes
• Managing studio session bookings
• Informing you about our services (with your consent)
privacy.s3text: Your personal data is retained for the duration necessary for managing our business relationship and in compliance with applicable legal obligations. Prospect data is retained for 3 years from the last contact.
privacy.s4text: Global Drip Studio does not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for commercial purposes. Your data may only be shared with our technical service providers strictly within the scope of performing our services.
privacy.s5text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, modification, disclosure, or destruction.
privacy.s6text: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights:
privacy.s6items:
• Right of access to your personal data
• Right to rectification of inaccurate data
• Right to erasure of your data
• Right to restriction of processing
• Right to data portability
• Right to object to processing
privacy.s6contact: To exercise these rights, contact us at: globaldripstudio@gmail.com
privacy.s7text: This website may use technical cookies necessary for its proper functioning. These cookies do not collect personal data for advertising purposes.
privacy.s8text: Global Drip Studio reserves the right to modify this privacy policy at any time. Changes will take effect upon their publication on this website.
privacy.s9text: For any questions regarding our privacy policy:
privacy.s9address: Address: 8 allée des ajoncs, 13500 Martigues, France

cgv.s1text: These Terms and Conditions (T&C) govern the contractual relationship between Global Drip Worldwide, hereinafter referred to as "the Studio," and any professional or non-professional client, hereinafter referred to as "the Client," for audio services offered by the Studio. Any order implies full and complete acceptance of these T&C, except for special conditions stipulated in writing and signed by both parties.
cgv.s2intro: The Studio offers the following services:
cgv.s2mixingDesc: balancing, tonal corrections, dynamics, spatialization, scene coherence.
cgv.s2mixmasterDesc: final output optimization and broadcast preparation.
cgv.s2masteringDesc: analog/digital chain depending on the project, compliance with broadcast standards.
cgv.s2sounddesignDesc: creation/editing of effects, foley, textures, impacts, transitions, sonic branding.
cgv.s2directionDesc: editorial advice, structure, instrumentation, creative guidance.
cgv.s2note1: The exact content and deliverables (formats, stems, variants) are specified in the quote/purchase order.
cgv.s2note2: Unless otherwise stated, third-party licenses/software/voiceovers/libraries are not included and may incur additional costs.
s3items: Prices are listed in euros including VAT on the website and/or in quotes. "Quote-based" services are subject to a personalized commercial proposal.
A 50% deposit may be required upon ordering; the balance is due upon delivery of final files. No delivery without full payment.
Accepted payment methods: bank transfer, PayPal, cash.

Discount for early payment: none. In case of late payment (professional clients), late penalties are due by law at the increased legal rate, along with a fixed compensation of €40 for recovery costs (art. L441-10 C. com.).
Any additional taxes, duties, or international banking fees remain the Client's responsibility.
Exploitation rights and/or the license (see article 7) are only granted after full payment.
s4items: Acceptance of the quote/purchase order ("approved") by the Client (signature or electronic agreement).
Receipt of the deposit, if applicable.
Receipt of the necessary elements (assets, instructions, schedule).
cgv.s4text: The Client guarantees the provision of usable elements with the necessary rights. Any delay in transmission, change of brief, or non-compliant asset may impact the schedule and cost.
cgv.s5intro: Indicative delivery times (unless otherwise stated in the quote):
cgv.s5essentialDesc: 4 hours (subject to availability and compliant assets).
cgv.s5otherDesc: delivery time indicated in the quote.
cgv.s5text: The Studio informs the Client of any significant contingency. Delivery times are exclusive of Client validations/feedback and force majeure events.
cgv.s6defText: one revision = targeted adjustments (levels, EQ, dynamics, transitions, balances, micro-edits).
cgv.s6excludedText: change of brief, massive track replacement, new editing, addition of extra versions, arrangement/composition overhaul.
cgv.s6extra: Any additional revision is billed at a rate communicated in advance. No feedback within 7 calendar days after delivery constitutes validation of the deliverables.
cgv.s7_1text: The Client retains all rights to their pre-existing works and elements provided (images, logos, texts, videos, voices, pre-existing music, etc.) and guarantees the Studio the legitimacy of their use.
cgv.s7_2title: 7.2 Studio's Methods, Know-How and Source Files
cgv.s7_2text: The Studio retains full ownership of its methods, techniques, presets, banks, templates, as well as source files and projects (DAW sessions, individual tracks not delivered by default). Source file delivery may be subject to an option and dedicated pricing.
cgv.s7_3text: Under French law, commissioned works do not automatically transfer ownership. Unless otherwise stipulated in writing, creations made by the Studio (original composition, arrangement, original sound design, recordings and masters) remain its intellectual property and/or that of its authors and rights holders.
cgv.s7_4text: When the Studio acts as composer, arranger, or sound designer, the Client does not, by default, acquire ownership of said works or corresponding masters. The Client benefits only from an exploitation license in accordance with article 7.5, unless a specific written transfer is made in accordance with article 7.6.
cgv.s7_5text: Upon delivery and subject to full payment, the Studio grants the Client a non-exclusive, non-transferable, and non-sublicensable license, limited to:
s7_5items: the identified audiovisual project and its direct variations (durations/formats),
the agreed media/platforms (e.g., website, YouTube, brand social media, event broadcast),
the territory and duration agreed in the quote.
cgv.s7_5extra: Any other use (TV/radio, large-scale paid campaigns, games/applications, standalone reuse of tracks, substantial remix/adaptation, transfer to third parties, additional synchronizations) requires written agreement and additional compensation. Delivered masters cannot be exploited separately from the project without prior agreement.
cgv.s7_6text: Any transfer (total/partial, exclusive/non-exclusive) must be formalized in writing, signed by the parties, specifying for each mode of exploitation the duration, territory, media, scope, and compensation. The transfer is only acquired after full payment. The authors' moral rights (attribution, respect for the work) remain inalienable.
cgv.s7_7text: Third-party rights (voice actors, musicians, performers, phonogram producers, sound libraries, plugins, fonts, etc.) are subject to separate authorizations/licenses. Their cost, scope, and usage constraints are specified in the quote or, failing that, require a supplementary agreement.
cgv.s7_8text: Unless otherwise instructed in writing, the Studio may be credited on the project (e.g., "Sound design/Mix — Global Drip Studio (Guillaume Surget)"). The Client respects moral rights (attribution, integrity of works).
cgv.s7_9text: Unless a confidentiality/embargo clause is agreed in writing, and subject to the Client's prior agreement on excerpts, the Studio is authorized to cite the project and distribute reasonable excerpts for promotional purposes (website, portfolio, social media, business development).
s8clientItems: Before work begins: refund of the deposit minus 20% administrative fees.
After work begins: no refund of the deposit; work already performed remains due.
cgv.s8studioText: In case of force majeure or exceptional circumstances, refund of amounts received pro rata to work not performed, without further compensation.
cgv.s8extra: External costs incurred (rental, travel, third-party providers) remain due/borne by the canceling party if non-refundable.
s9items: The Studio provides its services according to best practices and a best-efforts obligation. It cannot be held liable for indirect/intangible damages (loss of revenue, reputation, data, delays attributable to third parties).
The Client is responsible for the quality of source files and for holding the rights to the works. The Studio does not guarantee the suitability of deliverables for uses not specified in the quote.
Technical compliance (loudness, true peak, formats) is delivered for explicitly targeted platforms/channels. Any post-delivery changes in standards may require a billable update.
cgv.s10text: The Studio undertakes to preserve the confidentiality of non-public information and content communicated by the Client during the project and for 3 years after its completion. Information already public or legitimately obtained from third parties is not considered confidential. Obligations may be adapted by a specific NDA.
cgv.s11text: In the event of a dispute, the parties shall seek an amicable solution in good faith within 30 days. Failing that, the courts of the Studio's registered office jurisdiction shall have sole competence. French law applies to these T&C.
cgv.s12address: Address: 8 allée des ajoncs, 13500 Martigues, France
cgv.s13text: The Client's personal data (contact, billing, exchanges) is processed for the purposes of contract execution, invoicing, and follow-up. Legal basis: contractual execution/legitimate interest. Retention period: duration of the contractual relationship + legal obligations (accounting).
cgv.s13rightsText: access, rectification, erasure, restriction, objection, portability (within legal limits). Requests should be sent to the email indicated in article 12.
cgv.s13sub: The Studio may use subcontractors (hosting providers, collaborative tools) offering adequate guarantees; the main list can be provided upon request.
cgv.s14text: The Studio may, when necessary, entrust all or part of a service to specialized collaborators/subcontractors (voiceover artists, musicians, engineers, colorists, etc.) under its responsibility, without prejudice to the Client, in compliance with confidentiality and quality commitments.
cgv.s15text: Unless otherwise stated, the following are not included: travel/accommodation/per diem expenses, rental of third-party equipment/studios, remuneration of artists/performers, music licenses/libraries, neighboring rights, broadcast/clearance costs.
cgv.s15fieldText: the Client ensures access, filming permits, safety, and necessary insurance. Waiting times/delays not attributable to the Studio may be billed.
s16items: Unless otherwise stipulated, the master is delivered in stereo WAV 24-bit/48 kHz; other formats (MP3, multi-output, stems, M&E, social versions) upon request/quote.
A quality control report (loudness/true peak) may be provided when relevant.
cgv.s16archiveText: the Studio retains sessions and assets on a reasonable best-efforts basis for 90 days after delivery; beyond that, no guarantee of retention. An archiving/redelivery service may be offered as an option.
cgv.s17text: The Client agrees not to remove or alter agreed credits. Any communication by the Studio about the project respects any embargoes/confidentiality clauses.
cgv.s18text: The Studio reserves the right to amend these T&C at any time. The applicable T&C are those in effect at the date of acceptance of the quote by the Client.
